## TOOL USAGE:
@tool DSL SCHEMA
===
Description:
@read: read files. One file per line. Ranges are optional. Use start-end for line ranges, Hn for the first n lines, and Tn for the last n lines.
@grep: search text. Case-insensitive by default. Use | for multiple queries. Optional path or include=glob list. Query text is plain text only. This is code-first search, so common noise such as docs, text files, assets, generated files, and locales may be excluded unless you opt in with include=.
@find: find files or patterns. Use | for multiple names or patterns. Optional path.
@ls: list files.
@bash: run a shell command. Optional path.
@web: search the web.
@fetch: fetch a URL.
Ask: semantic code search.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
@edit: edit files. Multi-block edits are preferred. Partial edits can go through in multi-block edits when blocks do not overlap.
@write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.
Browser: browser/computer-use actions are outside this DSL.
===

Syntax:
@read: "path[ranges?]"
@grep: "query|optional pipes?" "include=*.ts,*.tsx|optional path?" (include= is optional; split with | if you need both include and path. Use include= when searching text/docs/non-code files)
@find: "pattern,optional commas?" "optional path?"
@ls: "path"
@bash: "command" "optional path?"
@web: "query"
@fetch: "url"
@ask: "query"
@agent: "prompt"
@edit: "path"
otxt[range?]: Old content
ntxt: New content

[More otxt/ntxt blocks?]
etxt
@write: "path"
Content goes here
etxt

@todo: "title?"
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item
ETXT
===

Execution & Rules:
- Use prose when no tools are needed.
- Use only tool calls when tools are needed.
- Tool mode begins when the first non-whitespace line starts with @name:
- Tool lines start at column 1 as @name:
- Only a top-level line starting at column 1 as @name: is a tool call.
Invald: Let me read this file @read: "file.ts"
Valid: Let me read this file
@read: "file.ts"
=== Escaping
- Inline mentions like "use @read for files" inside a normal sentence are just text.
- If you place a tool line on its own top-level line while discussing or demonstrating the tools, escape it with a leading / so it stays literal.
- Escape literal top-level tool lines in prose or content with a leading /, such as /@read: "file.ts" or /@server_tool: {"key":"value"}.
- Escape a literal etxt or EOF line in prose or content as /etxt or /EOF.
- If you are discussing, explaining, or showing the tool syntax itself, always escape those tool lines and etxt/EOF markers.
=== Using tools and closing
- After tool mode begins, every top-level non-empty line must start with @, unless it is body content for the active block or the etxt/EOF line closing @edit/@write/@todo.
- Use otxt/ntxt/etxt as the primary edit/write protocol.
- Use etxt as the primary closer for @edit, @write, and @todo. EOF is still accepted for compatibility, but etxt is preferred.
- Use otxt[1-3]: when you want a line range, or otxt: with no range.
- Use ntxt: for replacement text.
- otxt/ntxt blocks may replace a full block or just a substring within a line. You do not need to include the entire old line when the target text is unambiguous.
- After etxt or EOF, either start another top-level @tool line or end the response immediately.
- If the next top-level non-empty line is not @..., end the response instead of writing prose.
- If etxt/EOF is missing, the block may still close at the next top-level tool line or end of response as parser fallback, but you must not rely on that fallback.
=== Edit & Write stripping
- Successful @edit calls may later appear in conversation history as "Content placed in paired result below" placeholders.
- Successful @write calls may later appear as truncated previews ending with "..... see result for rest of write".
- Those placeholders do not mean the tool did nothing; the canonical applied edit blocks or post-write file snapshot are stored in the paired tool result.
- When history shows either form, treat the paired tool result as the source of truth for what changed.

- The only thing allowed after a tool call, is another tool call or the end of the response.
- If you include prose after a tool call, the response will be rejected.
- @grep is optimized for code search, not raw grep. If the target may be in .txt, .md, docs, assets, generated files, or similar non-code content, add an explicit include= glob that matches those files.
- Read before edit unless the exact target and/or context is already in recent tool results.
- Batch independent calls only.
- Do not batch dependent calls. Such as editing a file in the same turn as reading but that read isnt even in your context yet, so you're assuming what the contents are.
- Do not batch speculative edits with discovery.
- Stop using tools when the task is complete.
===

Flow:
- Map -> @ls or @find
- Search -> @grep (grep is your most important tool for discovery and likely will get you to where you need to be 99% of the time) or @ask
- Read -> @read (prefer specific line ranges over full file reads)
- Change or identify -> Identify/deduce problem, @edit, or @write the solution

1â†’# Lucky5 Clone-Parity Major Upgrade Plan
2â†’
3â†’Date: 2026-04-05
4â†’Status: Refined execution brief
5â†’Source baseline: `docs/LUCKY5_SUCCESSOR_CLONE_PARITY_PLAN_2026-04-05.md`
6â†’
7â†’## Summary
8â†’
9â†’This plan turns the clone-parity brief into an implementation-ready sequence that is faithful to the current Lucky5 repo.
10â†’
11â†’The goal is to bring Lucky5 to clone-level visual and interaction quality while preserving:
12â†’
13â†’- the clean-room backend
14â†’- the Lebanese cabinet identity
15â†’- the current machine-credit and wallet semantics
16â†’- the current double-up rules and payout architecture
17â†’
18â†’This is a **presentation and shell parity** upgrade, not a rules rewrite.
19â†’
20â†’## Current State Summary
21â†’
22â†’| Area | Vanilla JS (`server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/`) | React (`src/web/`) |
23â†’|------|-----------------------------------------------|--------------------|
24â†’| Shell screens | auth, lobby, wallet, admin, game | auth + in-cabinet machine selection only |
25â†’| Cabinet layout | portrait cabinet stack | dashboard-like cabinet plus side telemetry |
26â†’| Card rendering | clone PNG assets | CSS-rendered text cards |
27â†’| Control deck | clone button art + menu overlay | generic styled controls |
28â†’| Jackpot block | close to clone | simplified strip |
29â†’| Double-up viewport | trail-in-stage viewport | simplified side panel |
30â†’| Menu overlay | present | absent |
31â†’| Realtime | SignalR machine sync wired | no equivalent cabinet sync path |
32â†’
33â†’## Execution Stance
34â†’
35â†’The repo still declares the React cabinet as the active development target, but the vanilla JS cabinet is both the shipped playable surface and the closest clone-faithful implementation.
36â†’
37â†’The correct execution strategy is:
38â†’
39â†’1. use the vanilla JS cabinet as the **visual oracle and first parity surface**
40â†’2. validate layout, labels, shell flow, and pace there
41â†’3. then port the validated structure into the React cabinet as the long-term target
42â†’
43â†’This is a deliberate sequencing choice, not a change in product direction.
44â†’
45â†’## Non-Negotiable Guardrails
46â†’
47â†’Do not change:
48â†’
49â†’- Lebanese paytable semantics
50â†’- Two Pair minimum qualifying hand
51â†’- Ace-always-wins double-up behavior
52â†’- Lucky 5 switch-only no-lose behavior and multipliers
53â†’- deterministic pre-shuffled outcome logic
54â†’- backend authority for balances, machine state, jackpots, and double-up outcomes
55â†’- retro cabinet visual philosophy
56â†’
57â†’Do not accidentally redesign:
58â†’
59â†’- the machine-credit versus wallet model
60â†’- jackpot values entering machine credits
61â†’- `TAKE SCORE` and `TAKE HALF` settlement behavior
62â†’- REST-driven gameplay flow
63â†’
64â†’SignalR can remain a sync layer for machine state and fanout. Gameplay actions stay REST-driven unless explicitly changed later.
65â†’
66â†’## Phase 0: Foundation And Audit
67â†’
68â†’### 0.1 Asset inventory
69â†’
70â†’- Verify clone button images under `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/assets/images/` and `.../buttons/`
71â†’- Verify all 52 card PNGs plus `bside.png`
72â†’- Verify `board.png`, `lucky5.png`, `splash.png`, `coin.png`
73â†’- Verify `ARCADE.ttf` and current pixel-font fallback usage in `game.css`
74â†’- Catalog any still-missing APK shell assets from:
75â†’  - `New folder/tools/APK_Toolkit_by_0xd00d/3 - Extracted/app-arm64-v8a-release.apk/assets/flutter_assets/assets/images`
76â†’
77â†’### 0.2 Display contract audit
78â†’
79â†’- Confirm existing backend DTO coverage:
80â†’  - `JackpotInfoDto` already has `FullHouse`, `FullHouseRank`, `FourOfAKindA`, `FourOfAKindB`, `ActiveFourOfAKindSlot`, `StraightFlush`
81â†’  - `DoubleUpResultDto` already has `Status`, `DealerCard`, `ChallengerCard`, `SwitchesRemaining`, `IsNoLoseActive`, `LuckyMultiplier`
82â†’  - `MachineSessionDto` already has `MachineCredits`, `CashOutThreshold`, `CanCashOut`, `IsMachineClosed`, `WalletBalance`
83â†’- Treat `duCardTrail`, Lucky5 flash state, and similar history as **frontend display state first**
84â†’- Do not add backend DTO fields unless a parity requirement cannot be rendered from existing data
85â†’
86â†’### 0.3 Baseline verification
87â†’
88â†’- Run:
89â†’  - `dotnet build server/Lucky5.sln -v minimal`
90â†’  - `dotnet run --project server/tests/Lucky5.Tests/Lucky5.Tests.csproj`
91â†’- Record current screenshots before layout work begins
92â†’
93â†’## Phase 1: Cabinet Coordinate System
94â†’
95â†’Target: one fixed portrait cabinet face.
96â†’
97â†’- Refactor `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/css/game.css` so the game screen behaves as a single `720x1280` portrait board
98â†’- Preserve this vertical map:
99â†’  - `0%-15%`: paytable + credit/stake
100â†’  - `15%-19%`: contextual label band
101â†’  - `19%-38%`: card stage
102â†’  - `38%-42%`: large win row
103â†’  - `42%-53%`: jackpot + machine-info block
104â†’  - `53%-67%`: hold lamps
105â†’  - `67%-84%`: main controls
106â†’  - `84%-97%`: bottom controls
107â†’- Desktop should scale the cabinet as one object with surrounding negative space
108â†’- Do not reflow playfield controls into side columns
109â†’
110â†’Verification:
111â†’
112â†’- screenshot at portrait baseline
113â†’- idle, hold, win, and double-up states all remain on the same machine face
114â†’
115â†’## Phase 2: Label System And Text Hierarchy
116â†’
117â†’Target: labels become machine components, not incidental UI text.
118â†’
119â†’### 2.1 Paytable
120â†’
121â†’- Match clone row colors as closely as the evidence supports
122â†’- Keep solid highlight box on the selected jackpot row
123â†’- Keep active hand highlighting distinct from jackpot selection
124â†’- Keep payout values stake-sensitive
125â†’
126â†’### 2.2 Top-right counters
127â†’
128â†’- `CREDIT`: green label, white value below
129â†’- `STAKE`: amber label, white value below
130â†’- lock top-right position
131â†’
132â†’### 2.3 Machine-info block
133â†’
134â†’- Preserve:
135â†’  - `SERIE - n`
136â†’  - `KENT /3 : n`
137â†’  - `4K A / SF / 4K B`
138â†’  - FH meter with selected rank
139â†’  - `S/N`
140â†’  - `4 OF A KIND WINS BONUS`
141â†’- Keep FH meter visually distinct:
142â†’  - black text and numbers inside white container
143â†’  - positioned directly beneath the lower paytable area, matching current vanilla cabinet direction
144â†’
145â†’### 2.4 Contextual label bands
146â†’
147â†’- Keep `LUCKY 5 IS ACTIVE` between paytable and cards
148â†’- Keep hold prompt language in the playfield, not in side UI
149â†’- Keep bottom info-bar messages centered:
150â†’  - `INSERT COIN`
151â†’  - `PLAY 5000`
152â†’  - win and collection messages
153â†’- Keep double-up label band:
154â†’  - `HI LO GAMBLE`
155â†’  - `ACE COUNTS HI OR LO`
156â†’  - Lucky5 rule text
157â†’
158â†’Verification:
159â†’
160â†’- side-by-side screenshot check against clone references for:
161â†’  - placement
162â†’  - readability
163â†’  - color roles
164â†’  - no overlaps
165â†’
166â†’## Phase 3: Card Stage And Choreography
167â†’
168â†’Target: fixed card columns with faithful motion.
169â†’
170â†’- Keep five fixed base-game card slots
171â†’- Align each card vertically with:
172â†’  - paytable reading position
173â†’  - hold lamp
174â†’  - deck control slot
175â†’- Deal animation:
176â†’  - left to right
177â†’  - `90-120ms` stagger
178â†’  - drop-in from above
179â†’- Draw animation:
180â†’  - only unheld cards change
181â†’  - held cards stay physically stable
182â†’  - replacement timing `80-100ms`
183â†’- Use clone PNG cards only
184â†’- Use hold lamp assets, not generic CSS pills
185â†’
186â†’Verification:
187â†’
188â†’- deal one hand and verify left-to-right cadence
189â†’- hold selected cards and verify non-held-only replacement
190â†’
191â†’## Phase 4: Control Deck Rebuild
192â†’
193â†’Target: the lower third reads as cabinet hardware, not web buttons.
194â†’
195â†’- Use clone button assets for:
196â†’  - HOLD
197â†’  - BIG
198â†’  - SMALL
199â†’  - CANCEL HOLD
200â†’  - DEAL DRAW
201â†’  - BET
202â†’  - TAKE HALF
203â†’  - TAKE SCORE
204â†’- Preserve Lebanese button color semantics:
205â†’  - DEAL DRAW red
206â†’  - BET green
207â†’- Keep physical order:
208â†’  - hold row
209â†’  - BIG / SMALL / CANCEL HOLD / DEAL DRAW / BET
210â†’  - TAKE HALF / MENU / TAKE SCORE
211â†’- MENU remains a dark circular machine-mounted control
212â†’- Pressed state should use `_on` asset variants where available
213â†’
214â†’Verification:
215â†’
216â†’- all actions visible
217â†’- all pressed states work
218â†’- no button order drift
219â†’
220â†’## Phase 5: Win Amount And Jackpot Block
221â†’
222â†’Target: clone-faithful center scoring stack.
223â†’
224â†’- Keep large gold win amount directly beneath cards
225â†’- Keep A/B slot tag immediately beside the amount for relevant jackpot outcomes only
226â†’- Preserve current live jackpot block ordering from the vanilla cabinet
227â†’- Preserve active 4K slot highlight and FH rank presentation
228â†’
229â†’Important constraint:
230â†’
231â†’- `machineSerial`, `machineSerie`, and `machineKent` are not authoritative backend fields today
232â†’- For this phase, keep parity using the current vanilla approach unless a later explicit backend task formalizes them
233â†’- Do not claim these are already backend-tracked values
234â†’
235â†’Verification:
236â†’
237â†’- jackpot counters update
238â†’- active slot highlight changes correctly
239â†’- slot tag does not duplicate jackpot amount text
240â†’
241â†’## Phase 6: Double-Up Viewport Rebuild
242â†’
243â†’Target: retain clone-style double-up staging without unnecessary backend coupling.
244â†’
245â†’- Keep double-up inside the main card stage
246â†’- Preserve the current legacy model:
247â†’  - local `duCardTrail`
248â†’  - one active challenger slot
249â†’  - one visible page of trail cards
250â†’- Keep labels:
251â†’  - `BIG / SMALL ?`
252â†’  - revealed `BIG` and `SMALL`
253â†’  - gold dealer emphasis
254â†’  - Lucky5 glow or lightning-like flash on `5S`
255â†’- Use the current `DoubleUpResultDto.Status` and card fields to drive presentation
256â†’
257â†’Default rule:
258â†’
259â†’- do **not** add `CardTrail` or `IsLucky5Active` to backend DTOs in this phase
260â†’- only add backend fields later if the current frontend model proves insufficient for parity
